what_it_does: This file is a Claude Code plugin manifest for a package named 'recollection'; it declares the plugin's metadata, a referenced agent document, and an MCP server entry point.
observations: Version is 1.4.2 and the description claims cross-session, corpus-wide memory for Claude Code.
Author is Jesse Vincent with contact jesse@fsck.com.
homepage and repository are both set to local:///home/tim/recollection.
License is MIT and keywords include memory, search, semantic-search, and recollection.
The agents array lists a single relative file: ./agents/search-conversations.md.
mcpServers defines one server named 'recollection' that runs node with args pointing to ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/cli/mcp-server-wrapper.js and an empty env object.
standouts: The homepage and repository are hardcoded local file URLs under /home/tim.
The MCP server command uses a ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} placeholder variable inside a JSON string.
The plugin couples a manifest, an agent markdown file, and an MCP wrapper script as its integration surface.
conventions: Follows a JSON package/manifest shape similar to npm with name, version, description, author, license, keywords, and homepage.
Uses relative paths for plugin-local resources (agent doc and CLI wrapper).
MCP server entry is declared with command, args, and env keys.
concerns: local:///home/tim/recollection is a non-portable, user-specific path for homepage and repository.
${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} is not standard JSON interpolation and its resolution mechanism is not defined in this file.
The env object is empty, so any required runtime environment variables are not declared here.
notes: This manifest wires a memory plugin to Claude Code through an agent markdown file and an MCP server wrapper, but it hardcodes local paths and an unresolved environment variable.
questions: What behavior and prompts are defined in ./agents/search-conversations.md?
Are local:///home/tim/recollection URLs intended as placeholders or final values?
What Node.js version or dependencies does cli/mcp-server-wrapper.js require?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide cross-session memory and search for Claude Code, and the file does declare the plugin metadata plus the MCP server and agent entry points that would support that purpose; they align.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to package a persistent memory backend as a first-class Claude Code plugin, exposing search/recall both through an agent instruction file and an MCP server wrapper.
novelty: The combination of an agent markdown file and an MCP server wrapper in a single plugin manifest for conversation memory is distinctive; the local-only homepage/repository paths are also unusual.
contribution: This file contributes the integration contract for the recollection plugin: it names the package, declares its license and keywords, points to the agent documentation, and wires the MCP server wrapper so a fused system knows how to load and invoke the memory subsystem.
summary_for_embedding: A Claude Code plugin manifest for 'recollection' v1.4.2 that describes a cross-session memory tool, references an agent markdown file, and configures an MCP server wrapper via Node.js.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to register the recollection memory plugin with Claude Code and provide the metadata and entry points needed to expose search and recall functionality across sessions.
